name: Westside / Proctor Creek
neighborhoodType: Historic neighborhood near combined drainage pressure
runoffSource: Residential streets, curb inlets, parking lots, and low-lying blocks draining toward Proctor Creek

sourceEvidence.complaintDataset: Demo layer patterned after Atlanta 311 drainage/flooding complaint fields
sourceEvidence.complaintSummary: Clustered drainage complaints indicate repeated street ponding and inlet capacity issues near the Proctor Creek drainage area.
sourceEvidence.waterSupplyReason: High impervious cover and repeated complaints mean this area can generate enough runoff during storms to supply nearby bioswales, wetlands, and creek recharge.

name: South Downtown
neighborhoodType: Dense commercial district with paved corridors
runoffSource: Downtown roofs, plazas, viaduct streets, parking decks, and compact commercial blocks

sourceEvidence.complaintDataset: Demo layer patterned after Atlanta 311 drainage/flooding complaint fields
sourceEvidence.complaintSummary: High complaint count plus dense impervious cover flags rapid runoff toward downtown drains and viaduct low points.
sourceEvidence.waterSupplyReason: Large paved and roof surfaces create quick runoff volumes that can be captured for permeable pavement, storage, and headwater recharge.

name: Old Fourth Ward
neighborhoodType: Mixed-use district with park storage opportunities
runoffSource: Mixed-use roofs, BeltLine-adjacent pavement, streets, and park-edge runoff

sourceEvidence.complaintDataset: Demo layer patterned after Atlanta 311 drainage/flooding complaint fields
sourceEvidence.complaintSummary: Drainage complaints and dense redevelopment pressure suggest runoff should be routed to existing park detention capacity first.
sourceEvidence.waterSupplyReason: The neighborhood has enough pavement and roof area to feed the existing Historic Fourth Ward Park detention system.

name: Vine City / English Avenue
neighborhoodType: Residential area with known stormwater vulnerability
runoffSource: Residential streets, small lots, community facilities, and low points draining toward Proctor Creek tributaries

sourceEvidence.complaintDataset: Demo layer patterned after Atlanta 311 drainage/flooding complaint fields
sourceEvidence.complaintSummary: Frequent drainage complaints and high elevation-risk score indicate vulnerable residential runoff pathways.
sourceEvidence.waterSupplyReason: Because runoff originates close to homes, routing water into Cook Park, bioswales, and tributary recharge can reduce flood exposure while keeping water local.

name: Midtown Core
neighborhoodType: High-rise district with heat and runoff pressure
runoffSource: High-rise roofs, parking decks, wide streets, sidewalks, and compact commercial parcels

sourceEvidence.complaintDataset: Demo layer patterned after Atlanta 311 drainage/flooding complaint fields
sourceEvidence.complaintSummary: Moderate complaint count combined with very high impervious cover points to rapid runoff and heat-stressed streets.
sourceEvidence.waterSupplyReason: Dense roof and pavement surfaces can generate capturable runoff for Piedmont Park trees, Clear Creek recharge, and building-scale storage.

name: East Atlanta
neighborhoodType: Rolling residential district with creek-fed drainage
runoffSource: Neighborhood streets, residential roofs, community gardens, and creek-side drainage paths

sourceEvidence.complaintDataset: Demo layer patterned after Atlanta 311 drainage/flooding complaint fields
sourceEvidence.complaintSummary: Lower but still meaningful drainage complaints show localized creek-fed flooding and runoff opportunities.
sourceEvidence.waterSupplyReason: Rolling terrain can move runoff toward parks and creek channels, making it suitable for smart storage, rain gardens, and Sugar Creek recharge.

name: Airport / South River Headwaters
neighborhoodType: Large paved surfaces near headwater recharge zones
runoffSource: Large paved service areas, industrial parcels, logistics lots, and airport-adjacent drainage corridors

sourceEvidence.complaintDataset: Demo layer patterned after Atlanta 311 drainage/flooding complaint fields
sourceEvidence.complaintSummary: Large impervious surfaces near headwater areas create high-volume runoff that can overwhelm downstream channels.
sourceEvidence.waterSupplyReason: Large paved catchments can provide significant captured water for South River wetlands, retention cells, and drought baseflow refill.
